Ukraine Conflict News





14 February 2026 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

  • U.S. Forces Continue Strikes on ISIS Targets in Syria CENTCOM 14 Feb 2026 -- U.S. Central Command (CENTCOM) forces conducted 10 strikes against more than 30 ISIS targets in Syria, Feb. 3 - 12, to sustain relentless military pressure on remnants from the terrorist network.

  • US military kills 3 in attack on boat in the Caribbean Press TV 14 Feb 2026 -- The US military has killed three more people in a fresh attack targeting a boat in the Caribbean Sea, as it continues a deadly campaign that has killed at least 133 people since September 2025.

  • Coast Guard, partners seize 2 vessels with $5.6 million in illicit narcotics off Fort Lauderdale US Coast Guard 14 Feb 2026 -- A U.S. Coast Guard Station Fort Lauderdale law enforcement boat crew along with Customs and Border Protection Office of Field Operations officers and Homeland Security Investigations agents seized approximately 745 pounds of cocaine worth an estimated $5.6 million, from two suspected drug smuggling vessels off Port Everglades.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Rubio Wraps Warnings In Warmth As Europe Breathes A Sigh Of Relief RFE/RL 14 Feb 2026 -- In a hall packed with political and military leaders from across Europe and beyond, US Secretary of State Marco Rubio delivered a speech that managed to do two things at once: reassure anxious allies and issue a stark call for change.
  • Rubio: US And Europe 'Belong Together' RFE/RL 14 Feb 2026 -- In a speech at the Munich Security Conference, US Secretary of State Marco Rubio has said that the United States and Europe "belong together."
  • All Eyes On Rubio: Munich Braces For Transatlantic Reality Check RFE/RL 14 Feb 2026 -- The first day of the Munich Security Conference (MSC) ended late on February 13 with the world's foreign-policy elite left in a state of suspended animation.
  • At Munich Conference, Merz Urges US, Europe To 'Repair And Revive' Transatlantic Trust RFE/RL 14 Feb 2026 -- German Chancellor Friedrich Merz opened the Munich Security Conference by describing a geopolitical landscape bereft of its former signposts, saying the old world order "no longer exists," warning of threats posed by Russia and China, and suggesting US-Europe ties must be fixed to address the challenges.
  • Munich Security Conference kicks off with transatlantic rifts in focus People's Daily 14 Feb 2026 -- The 62nd Munich Security Conference (MSC) kicked off on Friday against the backdrop of deepening transatlantic tensions and an increasingly volatile geopolitical landscape.

  • Denmark warns US attack on Greenland would spell end of NATO Press TV 14 Feb 2026 -- Denmark's Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen has reiterated that any attempt by the United States to seize Greenland would effectively mark the end of the NATO military alliance.

  • Secretary of the Navy John C. Phelan Remarks at 2026 WEST US Navy 14 Feb 2026

  • Russia Warns NATO Military Preparations in Scandinavia Threaten National Security Sputnik 14 Feb 2026 -- Military preparations by Norway, Sweden and other NATO countries pose a direct threat to Russia's national security and compel Moscow to take military and technical measures in response, Russian Ambassador to Oslo Nikolai Korchunov said in an interview with Sputnik.

  • Volodymyr Zelenskyy and Mark Rutte Discussed Proposals for the Development of the PURL Initiative President of Ukraine 14 Feb 2026
  • Czech Initiative and the Development of the PURL and SAFE Programs: Volodymyr Zelenskyy Met with Petr Pavel President of Ukraine 14 Feb 2026

  • UK Carrier Strike Group to deploy to North Atlantic to keep UK safe UK MOD 14 Feb 2026 -- UK will bolster regional security by deploying a Carrier Strike Group to the North Atlantic and High North
  • Enemy weapons detector in the hands of soldiers five years early UK MOD 14 Feb 2026 -- New acoustic weapon detection system will help keep soldier safe on the battlefield by helping to locate and target positions of enemy gunfire, mortars and explostions.

  • Canada strengthens Defence Cooperation with the Kingdom of Denmark Canada 14 Feb 2026 -- At the Munich Security Conference on Friday, the Honourable David J. McGuinty, Minister of National Defence, alongside Denmark's Minister of Defence Troels Lund Poulsen, his Faroese counterpart Sirið Stenberg and his Greenlandic counterpart Vivian Motzfeldt, signed a Canada-Denmark Defence Cooperation Memorandum of Understanding (MOU).

  • Meeting between Defense Minister Koizumi and High Representative of the European Union for Foreign Affairs and Security Policy, Vice-President of the European Commission Kallas Ministry of Defense of Japan 14 Feb 2026
  • Japan-Australia Defense Ministerial Meeting (Summary) Ministry of Defense of Japan 14 Feb 2026
  • Japan-Germany Defense Ministerial Meeting (Summary) Ministry of Defense of Japan 14 Feb 2026

  • Panel discussion at the Munich Security Conference 2026 European Commission 14 Feb 2026
  • Speech by President von der Leyen at the Munich Security Conference 2026 European Commission 14 Feb 2026

  • Norway and Germany sign defence arrangement Government of Norway 14 Feb 2026 -- The Defence Ministers signed a historic defence arrangement, known as the "Hansa Arrangement". - It is more important than ever that European countries work closely together on our shared security, said Defence Minister Sandvik.

  • B. Braže in Munich: Transatlantic Alliance stands united. Allies agree Europe must step up investment in conventional deterrence and do so at a faster pace Latvia MFA 14 Feb 2026 -- From 12 to 14 February 2026, the Minister of Foreign Affairs, Baiba Braže, attended the 62nd Munich Security Conference, where she spoke at several high-level discussions on current foreign and security policy challenges, had meetings with ministers and senior officials from Ukraine, the Baltic states, the Nordic region, and other countries.

  • The President met with the German Chancellor at the Munich Security Conference President of the Republic of Lithuania 14 Feb 2026 -- President Gitanas Nausėda met with German Federal Chancellor Friedrich Merz at the Munich Security Conference on Saturday. The Prime Ministers of Estonia and Latvia also participated in the meeting.
  • The President participates in the Munich Security Conference President of the Republic of Lithuania 14 Feb 2026 -- On Saturday, President Gitanas Nausėda participated in the panel discussion at the Munich Security Conference, "Holding the Line: Defending Europe and Supporting Ukraine."

  • Australian navy frigate in Zambales for interoperability drills Philippine News Agency 14 Feb 2026 -- An Anzac-class frigate of the Royal Australian Navy (RAN), HMAS Toowoomba (FFH-156), is docked at Naval Operating Base Subic in Zambales for interoperability exercises, the Philippine Fleet (PF) said on Saturday.
  • PA, US armies showcase long-standing alliance in high-level meet Philippine News Agency 14 Feb 2026 -- Ranking officials of the Philippine Army (PA) and the United States Army Pacific (USARPAC) have reiterated the strong bilateral alliance between the two services.

  • READOUT: Ranking Member Shaheen, Senator Tillis, Representative Jacobs Meeting with Finnish President Alexander Stubb US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 14 Feb 2026 -- Today, U.S. Senators Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH), Ranking Member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, Thom Tillis (R-NC), along with Representative Sara Jacobs (D-CA) met with Finnish President Alexander Stubb on the sidelines of the 2026 Munich Security Conference.
  • READOUT: Ranking Member Shaheen, Senators Tillis, Kelly, Representative Jacobs Meeting with Danish Prime Minister Frederiksen and Greenlandic Prime Minister Nielsen US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 14 Feb 2026 -- Today, U.S. Senators Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH), Ranking Member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, Thom Tillis (R-NC) and Mark Kelly (D-AZ) along with U.S. Representative Sara Jacobs (D-CA) met with Danish Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen and Greenlandic Prime Minister Jens-Frederik Nielsen on the sidelines of the 2026 Munich Security Conference.
  • READOUT: Ranking Member Shaheen, Senators Tillis, Coons, Murphy and Kelly, Representatives Himes and Jacobs Meeting with Syrian Foreign Minister al-Shaibani and Commander of Syrian Democratic Forces US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 14 Feb 2026 -- Today, U.S. Senators Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH), Ranking Member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, Thom Tillis (R-NC), Chris Coons (D-DE), Chris Murphy (D-CT) and Mark Kelly (D-AZ), along with U.S. Representatives Jim Himes (D-CT) and Sara Jacobs (D-CA) met with Syrian Foreign Minister Asaad al-Shaibani and Commander of Syrian Democratic Forces General Mazloum Abdi on the sidelines of the Munich Security Conference to discuss Syria's political transition and regional stability.

  • Secretary of State Marco Rubio at the Munich Security Conference US Dept. of State 14 Feb 2026
  • Idea of World Without Borders, Global Order Was 'Foolish' - Rubio Sputnik 14 Feb 2026 -- The idea of a world without borders and replacing national interests with a global order turned out to be 'foolish,' US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said on Saturday.
  • Secretary Rubio's Meeting with G7 Foreign Ministers US Dept. of State 14 Feb 2026
  • Secretary of State Marco Rubio with John Micklethwait of Bloomberg News US Dept. of State 14 Feb 2026
  • Secretary Rubio's Meeting with Syrian Foreign Minister al-Shaibani US Dept. of State 14 Feb 2026

Defense Industry

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

    Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots

  • Netanyahu alone responsible for October 7 'disaster': Former Shin Bet chiefs Press TV 14 Feb 2026 -- Thirty-six former Israeli officials, including five ex-heads of the spy agency Shin Bet, have said Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu bears sole responsibility for the military and intelligence failures that enabled the al-Aqsa Flood Operation on October 7, 2023.

  • Hamas denounces brutal torture of Palestinian prisoners as war crime IRNA 14 Feb 2026 -- The Palestinian resistance movement, Hamas, has condemned the Israeli treatment of Palestinian prisoners, describing the brutal torture of detainees as a clear war crime and a violation of international law.

  • Sheikh Qassem: Hezbollah foiled objectives of enemy's aggression Press TV 14 Feb 2026 -- The secretary-general of Hezbollah says the Lebanese resistance movement has frustrated the enemy's objective to destroy the group, despite losses in a recent confrontation.
  • Iran drawing US-Israeli enmity because it blocks 'Greater Israel' scheme: Hezbollah official Press TV 14 Feb 2026 -- Iran is drawing US and Israeli hostility because it is blocking the so-called "Greater Israel" scheme in the region, says a senior Hezbollah official.

  • Meloni Says Italy Will Join Trump's Board of Peace as Observer - Reports Sputnik 14 Feb 2026 -- Italy has received an invitation to join the US-led Board of Peace as an observer and plans to accept, Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni said on Saturday.

  • Gaza's civil defense says 8,000 bodies of genocide victims remain under rubble Press TV 14 Feb 2026 -- The civil defense in the Gaza Strip says thousands of bodies are still buried beneath the ruins of destroyed buildings across the Palestinian territory, as rescue teams struggle to reach neighborhoods devastated during the Israeli regime's war of genocide.

    • Other Conflicts

  • Israel must cease violations of Syrian sovereignty: Iran UN envoy IRNA 14 Feb 2026 -- Iran's Ambassador and Permanent Representatives to the United Nations Amir Saeid Iravani says the Israeli regime should comply with international law and cease its violations of Syrian sovereignty and territorial integrity.

  • 'Shocking in scale and brutality': UN warns of Sudanese paramilitary war crimes in El Fasher Press TV 14 Feb 2026 -- Sudan's paramilitary Rapid Support Forces (RSF) have unleashed "a wave of intense violence...shocking in its scale and brutality" during their final push to seize the besieged city of El Fasher, a new UN report says.

  • Situation in the Great Lakes region (14 February 2026) France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 14 Feb 2026 -- France welcomes the agreement by the President of the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), Félix Tshisekedi, for the implementation of a ceasefire as proposed by Angola to all the warring parties.

News Reports

  • Thousands of Yemenis face deportation as U.S. ends temporary protected status People's Daily 14 Feb 2026 -- Nearly 4,000 Yemenis will be affected by a U.S. decision to end Temporary Protected Status (TPS) for Yemenis in the United States, said a Yemeni official on Friday evening.

  • Funding lapse set to trigger US Department of Homeland Security shutdown Press TV 14 Feb 2026 -- The United States Department of Homeland Security is expected to enter a shutdown after lawmakers failed to pass legislation to keep the agency funded beyond Saturday midnight in Washington, D.C.

  • Russia Poisoned Navalny With Exotic Frog Toxin, European Countries Say RFE/RL 14 Feb 2026 -- The governments of five European countries on February 14 released a statement saying they are confident that the Russian opposition politician Aleksei Navalny was poisoned with a lethal toxin not found naturally in Russia - a poisoning that led to his death in a Russian prison two years ago.
  • UK confirms Russia poisoned Navalny in prison with rare toxin UK FCDO 14 Feb 2026 -- UK and partners are today exposing the tragic circumstances surrounding Alexei Navalny's death and have confirmed there was poison found in samples from his body.
  • Joint Statement by the UK, Sweden, France, Germany and The Netherlands on Alexei Navalny's death UK FCDO 14 Feb 2026 -- Joint Statement by the UK, Sweden, France, Germany and The Netherlands on Alexei Navalny's death.

  • Keynote Speech by H.E. Wang Yi at the 62nd Munich Security Conference Conversation with China PRC MofA 14 Feb 2026 -- Joining Hands to Set the Ship of History on the Right Course
  • Secretary Rubio's Meeting with Director of the Office of the CCP Central Foreign Affairs Commission and China's Foreign Minister Wang Yi US Dept. of State 14 Feb 2026
    • Wang Yi Meets with U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio PRC MofA 14 Feb 2026 -- On February 13, 2026 local time, Member of the Political Bureau of the CPC Central Committee and Foreign Minister Wang Yi met with U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio on the sidelines of the Munich Security Conference.
  • China, US agree to strengthen dialogue, cooperation in various fields Global Times 14 Feb 2026 -- Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi met with US Secretary of State Marco Rubio here Friday on the sidelines of the Munich Security Conference, with both sides agreeing to strengthen dialogue and cooperation in various fields and promote the stable development of China-US relations.
  • Chinese Foreign Minister meets European leader, top diplomats on sidelines of 2026 MSC; expert says intensive meetings highlight close and inseparable nature of China-Europe ties Global Times 14 Feb 2026 -- Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi, on the occasion of attending the 2026 Munich Security Conference (MSC) in Germany, met with European leader and a number of foreign ministers on the sidelines of the MSC, including Serbian President Aleksandar Vucic, Austrian foreign minister Beate Meinl-Reisinger, British Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper, German Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul and French Foreign Minister Jean-Noel Barrot, according to official release from the Chinese Foreign Ministry.
  • China and Europe are partners, not rivals, Chinese FM tells German and French counterparts in first-ever trilateral meeting Global Times 14 Feb 2026 -- The 50 years of interactions and cooperation between China and Europe have proven that the two sides are partners, not rivals. Mutual dependence is not a risk, intertwined interests are not a threat, and open cooperation does not harm security, Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi said during a trilateral meeting with German Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul and French Foreign Minister Jean-Noel Barrot, on the sidelines of the 2026 Munich Security Conference (MSC) on Friday, according to a readout released by the Chinese Foreign Ministry on Saturday.
  • Wang Yi Meets with French Minister for Europe and Foreign Affairs Jean-Noël Barrot PRC MofA 14 Feb 2026 -- On February 13, 2026 local time, Member of the Political Bureau of the CPC Central Committee and Foreign Minister Wang Yi met with French Minister for Europe and Foreign Affairs Jean-Noël Barrot on the sidelines of the Munich Security Conference.
  • Wang Yi Meets with German Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul PRC MofA 14 Feb 2026 -- On February 13, 2026 local time, Member of the Political Bureau of the CPC Central Committee and Foreign Minister Wang Yi met with German Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul on the sidelines of the Munich Security Conference.
  • German Chancellor Friedrich Merz Meets with Wang Yi PRC MofA 14 Feb 2026 -- On February 14, 2026 local time, German Chancellor Friedrich Merz met with Member of the Political Bureau of the CPC Central Committee and Foreign Minister Wang Yi in Munich.
  • Wang Yi Meets with UK Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper PRC MofA 14 Feb 2026 -- On February 13, 2026 local time, Member of the Political Bureau of the CPC Central Committee and Foreign Minister Wang Yi met with UK Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper on the sidelines of the Munich Security Conference.
  • Wang Yi Meets with Austrian Federal Minister for European and International Affairs Beate Meinl-Reisinger PRC MofA 14 Feb 2026 -- On February 13, 2026 local time, Member of the Political Bureau of the CPC Central Committee and Foreign Minister Wang Yi met with Austrian Foreign Minister Beate Meinl-Reisinger on the sidelines of the Munich Security Conference.
  • Serbian President Aleksandar Vučić Meets with Wang Yi PRC MofA 14 Feb 2026 -- On February 13, 2026 local time, Serbian President Aleksandar Vučić met with Member of the Political Bureau of the CPC Central Committee and Foreign Minister Wang Yi on the sidelines of the Munich Security Conference.

  • China warns US against 'plotting' on Taiwan, says it risks confrontation Press TV 14 Feb 2026 -- China's top diplomat has warned that any attempt by the United States to "plot" on Taiwan could trigger a direct confrontation between the world's two largest economies, escalating already fraught tensions over the self-ruled island.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 14 Feb 2026
  • Any separatist attempt to resist reunification nothing but wishful thinking: mainland spokesperson People's Daily 14 Feb 2026 -- A Chinese mainland spokesperson on Friday said that any attempt to split the country or resist reunification is nothing but wishful thinking.

  • Trump Says Regime Change 'Best Thing That Could Happen' In Iran RFE/RL 14 Feb 2026 -- US President Donald Trump said regime change is the "best thing that could happen" in Iran, as he confirmed that a second aircraft carrier would soon join a "massive" US armada in the Arabian Sea amid high tensions between the bitter rivals.
  • US Preparing for Possible Weeks-Long Campaign Against Iran - Reports Sputnik 14 Feb 2026 -- The US military is preparing for the possibility of sustained operations against Iran if President Donald Trump orders an attack, two US officials told Reuters.
  • Regime Change 'Would Be the Best Thing That Could Happen' - Trump on Iran Sputnik 14 Feb 2026 -- US President Donald Trump was asked whether he wants regime change in Iran and replied that it "seems like that would be the best thing that could happen," adding: "Let's see what happens."
  • Swiss to act as venue of next round of Iran-US talks: Report Press TV 14 Feb 2026 -- Switzerland has confirmed that it will act as the venue for a second round of indirect talks between Iran and the United States, as the two countries seek to settle disputes around Tehran's nuclear program.
  • Political pressure cannot undermine Iran's 'inalienable' enrichment right: Foreign Ministry Press TV 14 Feb 2026 -- Iran's "inalienable" right to enrichment cannot be undermined by political pressure, says the Foreign Ministry spokesman.
  • Minister Anand announces additional sanctions against Iran Canada 14 Feb 2026 -- Today, the Honourable Anita Anand, Minister of Foreign Affairs, announced that Canada is imposing additional sanctions against 7 individuals under the Special Economic Measures (Iran) Regulations (Iran regulations).
  • Backgrounder: Canada imposes additional sanctions against Iran Canada 14 Feb 2026 -- The Islamic Republic of Iran (Iran) continues to pose a serious threat to international peace and security through destabilizing activities across the Middle East and the world.
  • Bushehr Nuclear Plant's 2nd and 3rd units 18% complete, Iran says IRNA 14 Feb 2026 -- Iran says that the combined physical progress of the second and third units at the Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant had reached 18%, as construction work continued in line with project schedules.
  • Putin affirms Russia's support for Iran's defense for its sovereignty, interests IRNA 14 Feb 2026 -- Russian President Vladimir Putin, in a congratulatory message to his Iranian counterpart, Masoud Pezeshkian on the 47th anniversary of the victory of the Islamic Revolution, has affirmed his country's support for Iran.
  • Russia reaffirms support for Iran's sovereignty amid rising US threats Press TV 14 Feb 2026 -- President Vladimir Putin says Russia supports Iran's efforts to safeguard its sovereignty and interests amid mounting threats from the United States.
  • Iranian Opposition Holds Largest Ever Protest In Europe, With 250,000 People In Munich RFE/RL 14 Feb 2026 -- Some 250,000 people have joined a demonstration in the Bavarian capital, German police told RFE/RL, in the largest ever protest in Europe by opponents of the authorities in Iran.

  • PM meeting with President of the European Commission: 14 February 2026 Gov.UK 14 Feb 2026
  • PM speech during the Munich Security Conference: 14 February Gov.UK 14 Feb 2026

  • Minister Anand meets with Germany's Federal Minister for Foreign Affairs Canada 14 Feb 2026 -- The Honourable Anita Anand, Minister of Foreign Affairs, today met with Johann Wadephul, Germany's Federal Minister for Foreign Affairs, at the Munich Security Conference, held from February 13 to 15, 2026, in Germany.

  • Foreign Minister MOTEGI's Attendance at the Munich Security Conference Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 14 Feb 2026
  • Japan-Germany Foreign Ministers' Meeting Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 14 Feb 2026
  • Japan-U.S. Foreign Ministers' Meeting Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 14 Feb 2026
  • Secretary Rubio's Meeting with Japanese Foreign Minister Motegi US Dept. of State 14 Feb 2026
  • Meeting between Foreign Minister MOTEGI and NATO Secretary General Rutte Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 14 Feb 2026
  • The G7 Foreign Minister's Meeting Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 14 Feb 2026
  • Working dinner of the Foreign Ministers of the G4 Countries on UN Security Council Reform Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 14 Feb 2026

  • Bangladesh Nationalist Party wins 209 seats in national parliament election: election commission People's Daily 14 Feb 2026 -- Bangladesh Nationalist Party (BNP), led by Tarique Rahman, secured 209 out of 299 seats in the parliament following Thursday's general election, a senior official said Friday.

  • Canada welcomes Poland's accession to G7 Rapid Response Mechanism Canada 14 Feb 2026 -- The Honourable Anita Anand, Minister of Foreign Affairs, today met with Radosław Sikorski, Poland's Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Foreign Affairs, at the Munich Security Conference, held from February 13 to 15, 2026, in Germany.

  • Bangladesh: Statement by the Spokesperson on the elections European External Action Service (EEAS) 14 Feb 2026 -- The European Union welcomes the holding of genuinely competitive parliamentary elections and referendum in Bangladesh in a peaceful atmosphere.

  • Prime Minister Tusk: We need to be strong, we can't stop being good Republic of Poland 14 Feb 2026 -- During this year's Munich Security Conference, Prime Minister Donald Tusk delivered a laudatory speech for the recipient of the Ewald von Kleist Award, which this year was awarded to the heroic Ukrainian Nation.

  • Land Seizures Spread Across Uzbekistan As Farmers Cite Push For Chinese Capital RFE/RL 14 Feb 2026 -- A wave of land disputes that began in eastern Uzbekistan has spread to Sirdaryo, where farmers say authorities are using courts, debt enforcement, and administrative pressure to shift farmland into a system that favors foreign, particularly Chinese, investors.

  • Macron Committed to Doing Everything to Overthrow Niger Authorities - Nigerien President Sputnik 14 Feb 2026 -- French President Emmanuel Macron has committed to doing everything in his power to overthrow Niger's current authorities, Nigerien President Abdourahamane Tchiani said.

  • Secretary-General's remarks at the 39th African Union Summit UN Secretary-General 14 Feb 2026
  • Secretary-General's press conference at the 39th African Union Summit UN Secretary-General 14 Feb 2026
  • Guterres tells AU Summit: 'This is 2026 - not 1946' in push for reform UN News 14 Feb 2026 -- Calling for sweeping reforms of global institutions, UN Secretary-General António Guterres told African leaders on Saturday that the absence of permanent African seats on the Security Council is "indefensible," declaring: "This is 2026 - not 1946."
